title: Rotate Matrix In Place
prompt:
Given an `n x n` matrix `matrix`, rotate it **90 degrees clockwise**, in place, and return it.

**Example**: `rotate([[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]])` → `[[7,4,1],[8,5,2],[9,6,3]]`

Good spatial-reasoning-under-pressure practice — either transpose then reverse each row, or rotate layer-by-layer in groups of 4 cells.
hint_notes: Transpose (swap matrix[i][j] with matrix[j][i] for j > i, in place) then reverse every row — two simple O(n^2) passes. Layer-by-layer 4-way cell rotation is the alternative if asked for a single-pass approach. Tag: transpose-then-reverse.
